My niece is a trend setting tween. At 12 years old, she loves anything she can buy at Old Navy (her go to store) and the Gap. She doesn't always have the funds for it, so this was a perfect way to give her options and help the fashionista in her keep on point.

Being that she's a hip (yes i'm old) tweenster and her Aunt is old, I figured what better way to hook a girl up with an amazing Christmas present than to give her something she can use to pick out herself. Worked like a charm! I ended up buying two of these cards for a total of $50, she was thrilled that she can use them at more than just Old Navy. Options work wonders I have found with 12 year olds.

The cards came activated, complete with the receipt that shows they are live. They, like any other card, fit perfectly into her new wallet.

Anyhow, this girl went out shopping and has spent a portion of the money and i'm sure will spend more as the year progresses!

Haha, this is funny that I get to review my Amazon Gift Card. So this is my re-loadable gift card for all my Amazon purchases. I don't know about anyone else but I hate all the little charges that come up on my account for Amazon. It is nice that they don't charge till it ships but each product coming out one by one by one! Sometimes it is confusing, because I remember the full purchase of everything in my order and not the individual prices. So I have to go back and look and see, I have to be sure someone didn't get a hold of my card again, it has happened in the past!

So here is a solution I found. Just put one charge on my card for a gift card. It gets loaded right to my account and I can reload it when I need to. So no more little charges here and there adding up on my statement. Easy. So if that part of shopping with Amazon annoys you like it did me, here is a solution that will keep you shopping happy. I am just surprised that it is on my list of items to review!!! It tickles me to see it here, but hey maybe this will help someone else. :)

These cards are one of the best options to use for holiday gifts to people in the service industries around your kids’ education systems - bus drivers, matrons, aids to teachers, ancillary teach staff, school security guards, the list goes on. Giving cash somehow seems crass - culturally I am not used to just paying in cash as appreciation for someone’s work through the semester when it comes to near the holidays. Giving gifts is a hit or miss, mostly a miss - teachers and staff specifically mention how they are tired of receiving coffee mugs and teacher-branded cute showpieces and other general merchandise. Amazon cards are among the best options, particularly as the recipients can club them together and buy what they really want. The holiday theme, the lower denominations, and the multi-pack all go towards making the process easier.

Thanks to Amazon for perfecting the art of laziness. We have gone from searching for the perfect gift, balancing budgets / stores / promotions / coupons / crowds, to giving gift cards. Even that needed stepping out to physically go get them, but here we are, gift cards from the stores people love without having to leave the comfort of home.

I thought it would be a fun gift for my ex-college roommate. We share the same taste in music. NOT, NOT, NOT. If you buy it from the US and send the PIN overseas they won't honor it. Amazon says nothing about it, but when you get the card and your friend enters the PIN Spotify will not accept it. Then you read the fine print on the card and there it is... only you have to buy the card to get that part of the fine print. Even worse - I can't use the card for my own account because I have a family plan. This has been a two day dumpster fire so far. I gave it 3 stars because the one star reviews seem to be written by bots most of the time. But really... it's a zero star deal if you want to give a gift to an overseas friend.

Note: I love Spotify itself which is why I wanted to share it. I cannot understand why they make it so hard to give as a gift. I didn't want to go to Amazon or Target to buy it. I wanted to give my money to Spotify itself, but they are strangely obtuse about this simple thing.

[EDIT - When I contacted them Amazon called me back immediately and agreed with me that the posted information was misleading. In spite of the "no refunds" warning they reimbursed me that same day. Excellent customer service.]

I would attach a photo, but I’d have to blur out the gift card number, so just look at the photo on Amazon. If you are purchasing multiple gift cards with different values, there is nothing on gifting options to complete the specific card for individual recipients. So, if you choose to complete the gifting options and the person uses their receipt to return the card for some reason, it may or may not use the correct card.

Same problem when you received them. You may Ave a back with $1000 worth of gift cards and no indicator of the value of the individual cards. You can find it out if you go to your order online, since it will list the serial numbers of the cards. So, you’ll have to do some detective work, but then there is no place on the gift card holder to write the value, who it’s to and who it’s from. The cards are plain white, so you could write on it with a marker, but not the best. I ordered about a dozen of different values and was very disappointed and dissatisfied.

So, word of advice. Pick a different one from Amazon that has the value, or you can place the value somewhere. Order different values separately and wait a day between orders. Worked perfectly for me. Was able to use online, as credit and as debit. First thing when it arrived I made sure there was a slip included showing Amazon had activated it (PEOPLE: If this is not included it is a red flag and you must contact Amazon immediately... If it wasn't activated they just need to send you a new replacement card). I then went online immediately to the website on the card and the balance showed immediately and said the card was active. Then I set up my address and pin number on the same page and that was it... Less than 5 minutes. Many people leaving negative reviews must not understand how to register the card online. If you don't go online and add your address, you can't use online. If you don't go in and add a pin number, you can't use it as a debit. You also can't use it to get cash back (which the seller discloses), and I bet some people are just mad that's not an option. Just read the fine print, read the very simple instructions on the card, and you're good to go.

I love Amazon! I tend to always look here first before I purchase anything. Not just because Amazon has just about anything, but they have all the colors and the options that I could ever possibly want, including ones that I didn't even know I wanted until I saw them. In addition, I love being able to read through the customer reviews. I have been swayed away from items that I thought I wanted because of the reviews, and have instead purchased a similar item based on good reviews. I have had very good experiences by following those guidelines. I never have to fear when ordering through Amazon. As a prime member, I know when my items will arrive, I know that they will arrive on time, I know that they will be packaged well, and based on my reading through reviews? I pretty much know that I will enjoy the item that I receive.

There are so many reasons to purchase an Amazon gift card and to keep the balance loaded. It is so much easier to budget for your purchases, transfer gift card balances, or simply keep track of your Amazon spending.

In addition, they almost always have specials that they run if you load a certain amount. Since I frequently make purchases (including monthly subscriptions), this is the best way to keep my statements from being cluttered with several small Amazon purchases each week. Instead, there is a withdrawal for the gift card purchase, and then all the smaller purchases just come out of that.

Amazon also makes it so easy to do!

So, if you are buying something and you have a small limit on a credit card or want to use more than one credit card when purchasing something this is the way to do it. For the way I purchase this gift card reload feature is a major convenience. I have multiple sources of cards, and sometimes want to use two to three to make a single purchase. The ability to use a gift card comes in handy in those situations, so being able to reload meets my needs perfectly.

The way I use it for multiple cards is to reload from one card, then another until I have distributed the funding sources. It literally takes seconds per reload, and when I am using three or more cards, it can all be done in a minute or two. Then when I need to make a purchase the entire amount is waiting. This is much faster and more efficient than purchasing multiple gift cards.

Christmas 2016, I bought a gift card for my dad.  In 2017, his house burned down in the Tubbs Fire in Santa Rosa, and he lost everything.  I got him another gift card in 2017, and he told me he never got a chance to use the one I got him in 2016 before it burned in the fire.  I contacted Amazon to see if there was any way to replace the destroyed card.  The customer service rep was extremely helpful.  He was able to check that the card I bought in 2016 was never redeemed, and he ordered a replacement card immediately.  He gave us one day shipping for free and the card arrived the next day.  I can't really explain how happy and emotional my dad and I both were to receive the replacement card. When you have lost everything, something this simple means so much.  Thank you Amazon!!!

But when my Mom said she got one package and described it as a box..I was concerned and asked her if there was a 2nd package with a gift card.  No just one package..I told her it was delivered on Sunday the 22nd per tracking . Finally, she opened the package prior to Christmas because I had to tell her there was a monetary gift involved.  She opened it and threw the protective wrap in her outside trash and trash day was to be the next day.  She recovered it and opened up the wrap and there was the $100 Mastercard  Gift Card .about to be trashed.  Maybe separate packages would be a better way when 2 gifts are going same day to the same person..or something to indicate please make sure to open the wrap or package inside indicator..not sure just how, but something as this could have ended up so much worse.  Otherwise she loves the other gift and certainly loves the gift card.  Thanks

I bought this as a gift. It comes in a sealed package which is really nice. However, here is a word of advice: on the package itself, it states that the card is not valid until activated by a cashier. This had me a bit worried that I had just paid $50 for an unloaded gift card. I opened the package so that I could get the code off the back of the card and check the balance on the website provided. Thankfully, the card IS loaded with the $50. I wish I would have left it in the package it came in because it would've looked better as a gift, but I felt I had to make sure it was actually loaded first. So learn from my mistake- yes, the card is loaded. And if you really want to be sure it is loaded, it provides a website you can go to to check the card balance. However, if you're buying this as a gift and want to keep it in the packaging (looks nicer that way) then you can be rest assured that it is actually loaded and you don't have to open it to check.

My purchase was problem free but I also took the time to call the toll free number listed in information packet to check on the card balance. In my case the card was valid with funds. For this reason ONLY I gave it 5 stars. I however give VISA the brand “1” star because they have the POWER to implement changes that can eradicate all the issues around their pre-paid cards. It’s criminal and greedy to take advantage of consumers all because they have BRAND MONOPLY. Which basically means their name/brand carry enormous weight and clout that has afforded them a nice cushy spot as the leading source in pre-paid cards. Brand monoply allows for VISA something they otherwise could not afford to do because there are no other alternatives for us the consumer. There mindset is, “We don’t have to do anything but supply a card upon purchase...it doesn’t need to be dependable or reliable because we don’t have competiting brands that can match our years of branding and global recognition so we don’t really need to work too hard or break a sweat in providing quality service for our pre-paid cards...our integrity is competition based which means the less competition, less focus on ethics and integrity and more capitalization on being the monopoly!”

Unfortunately folks it’s a win win for VISA and crap shoot for us....so be cautious and aware of the game!...

I ordered a gift card for each of my children and grandchildren for Hannukah. I wrote a different (although similar) note to each and signed it from my spouse (a stepparent) and me. The first card arrived today to my middle daughter. The rest are arriving tomorrow for some reason. I received a rather confused call from my granddaughter asking me if I had sent her a gift card. I asked her why the confusion as to who the card was for. She sent me a photo of the enclosures. A generic note saying that it was from me and that it was for her mother (but she thought that perhaps I addressed the card to her mom for mail purposes). My note to my daughter was left off. My granddaughter thought that we had sent her $100 and it was all confusing. Tomorrow, she will hopefully get her $75 gift card and hopefully a note. But I resent paying as much as I did only to have Amazon ignore it and send a generic card. Very disappointing.
Update: Amazon lost my granddaughter’s gift card. They will replace it and made a point out of telling me that it will be at no charge to me! And, if all goes well, she will get it tomorrow, 5 days after her mother, aunts, uncle and cousins all got one. Good thing she is old enough to understand that its late arrival is no reflection on what we think of her!

I'll never say no to an Amazon gift card!

That said, I did have an issue with my purchase... The balance reload page offers several amount options (50/100/200/500), or a field to enter a different amount. I chose the option for $500 because I knew I would be making some sizeable purchases soon. I expected the balance to be reflected in my account within minutes, and the email I got confirming my order told me that's what would happen ("Typically your balance is updated within 5 minutes of your order.")

A few minutes later, it wasn't there. An hour later, it still wasn't there. A few hours later, it still wasn't there.

I contacted Amazon and was told that because of the size of the reload, there was extra verification needed on their end. This was surprising, because the reload page said nothing about this, the confirmation email contradicted it, and the amount I chose was one of the predetermined options.

So if $500 requires extra verification, maybe tell people that when they order.

After many years of using the service, get shown the door, even though I changed my password and deleted my payment information from the site so that could never possibly happen again. Stay away - no need to be a customer of a company that treats their customers like criminals.

I actually am a huge fan of Amazon Gift Card and also of the "Balance Reload." In the last 6 months I've been making a lot more purchases on Amazon than I did before (for a variety of reasons) and there are two things that I've noticed that kind of bothered me: A.) When looking at my credit card statement it always just says the amount, and that it was purchased via Amazon, so there are a ton of entries for Amazon purchases that don't really mean anything to me. B.) it's really easy to spend a lot of money because, well. . . you can find pretty much anything on Amazon and it's too easy to buy with things like 1-click purchasing, subscribe and save, etc.
Reloading a gift card balance means that I still get my rewards points for buying things on Amazon, but can also very easily set a budget for myself beforehand. Also, every-time that I buy something I'll be able to see how much I have left of that original amount. In short, if you buy a lot of things off of Amazon I would highly recommend using this feature. I still have some things that I buy through 1-click purchasing . . . but most everything else I use gift cards for.

My mother is a Kindle user, and so she often asks for Amazon gift cards for Christmas. This year I decided to go the easy route and ordered one to put in her stocking. It arrived very quickly and the card it included did have an inner envelope.

She was very pleased and it saved me time for shopping!

I was really excited about the “anytime” gift card concept. I can keep a stack of gift cards ready for birthdays or last-minute gifts but pay for them and decide on the amount only when I need them!

Unfortunately, Amazon seems to have launched this product without testing it. What a nightmare the first time I tried to load one! I eventually figured out a way to do it, so let me save you the trouble:

You need to scan the QR code on the back of the gift card. As far as I can tell, Amazon hasn’t released any software that allows us to do this. You could type in the extremely long serial number manually, but the card is glued to the envelope right over the serial number, so revealing that requires you to remove the card from its gift envelope, thus ruining the gift envelope.

After many internet searches and three(!) third-party apps, here’s what worked for me:

Using my iPhone, I downloaded the free QR code reader named simply “Scan” from the App Store. Use that app to scan the QR code on the back of the gift card. (It’s very difficult to do because the code is so small and not centered in the window. Some apps won’t scan a code this small, but Scan has been working for me.)

The app will take you to an Amazon.com page and request your user name and password. Don’t enter this now! (I doubt it’s a good idea to put your Amazon password into ANY free, third-party app...)

Click the Safari icon in the lower right corner to open that page in your phone’s browser. Then log in to Amazon using your user name and password.

Finally, you should be taken to a page where you can load a balance onto your gift card. Amazon will probably make you re-enter your credit card information for “security purposes,” so have your card ready.

The good news is that so far, I haven’t experienced any of the problems that other reviewers mentioned where the recipient couldn’t use the card because the balance had been applied to the *purchaser’s* account! (Ugh!)

I hope Amazon fixes these problems soon, because the idea is great and I’d find many reasons to use these gift cards if it were easier!

My pre-teenage and teenage grandchildren can be very difficult to buy gifts for. They are very vague on what they might want and you are never sure if a gift you find will be acceptable and wanted. I have had better luck gifting them an Amazon gift card that they can then use to purchase an item they desire later.  They place orders thru their parents accounts, but individually pick what they want. I have found their choices to be wise and the gift card well spent!

Nice way when I get extra fundage during month, I can tuck it onto here to be used for my monthly order of essentials at payday. Even $5 can be welcome when it comes to stocking up time, and it can't be wasted on something then (like fast food). An overlooked and useful item to help you keep your budget balanced, and not just your pantry stocked but other needfuls as well. At times Amazon also offers a small bonus for reloading a certain amount; or if you have to do a return/refund, the funds come back to you much quicker if you opt for a giftcard balance, ready for you to replace that item or purchase something else. Glad I found out about this and happy to use it.

I decided i should just give up and wait till i could get one from the store. Then just after I was about to give up, I came across this Petco gift card. These Amazon gift cards that come with a blank greeting card are extremely convenient! They usually arrive in 24-48 hours. I don't have to worry about buying a separate greeting card, since Amazon has some really nice greeting card designs for all occasions. The greeting cards are blank on the inside, but that doesn't bother me, as I prefer to personalize my greeting cards anyway. I also like how many of the gift cards themselves have a design on them as well, and that you can buy them in so many different denominations. If you buy them at the store, they usually only come in the one design with the "a" on it and have only 3 or 4 limited denominations that they can be purchased in. I've bought a couple of these for birthdays, holidays, and anniversaries. Most people I know buy something or another through Amazon.

Another option I like when I'm purchasing a last minute gift (like on Christmas Eve), is the option to purchase an Amazon gift card and have it emailed to you or the recipient. It isn't nearly as pretty, but it is a very convenient option with instantaneous delivery which also allows you to purchase in various different denominations. Both the greeting card and email options also save you from the hassle of running to a retail store and waiting on a long line to buy one or two gift cards during the holiday season.

These gift card tins are great. The diamond plate tin with a padded interior for the card is a nice little extra to affix to a birthday card and does not increase the cost of the gift card. Also, there is flexibility in gifting denomination as the giver can choose whatever amount they wish to gift; the only downside is that the actual physical gift card will not display the amount if you choose something other than the standard $10, $25, $50 denominations.
Couple the one-day shipping with the fact that these gift cards can be put toward just about anything the recipient could possibly want and these are a great option for a last minute gift or a gift for someone who is difficult to shop for.

I received my first Amazon gift card for Christmas to accompany my new Kindle Touch.  What a perfect accompaniment!  It made my husband's shopping so much easier and I was amazed at how easy Amazon made the process of using it.  Thanks for making my Christmas!

The container, yes, the container. I ordered it thinking it would be a nice place for people to drop their change, pins, buttons, whatever. But, Amazon cut a hole in the bottom and put in a cheap piece of plastic, making it much more fragile and less likely to last a long time, as a keep-sake should. What a waste!

This is a great gift to give during the Holiday season.  The color scheme is very ambiguous, so it can be either a Hanukkah or Christmas gift.  I personally used this as a gag gift for someone that lives in 120 degree weather in the peak of summer.  The container is a pretty high quality aluminum (I think its aluminum).  Even though it comes with one gift card, there is room to stuff maybe half a dozen more in there.  There is a view slot on the bottom side, so it does give the contents away, which may be unfortunate depending on the occasion.  Otherwise this is a very great gift with equally great presentation.

The aluminum case is a great bonus to the gift card.  Although the Gift card was immediately consumed, the case it came in can be repurposed for other cards.  You can fit about 5-10 additional cards in this case.

But they mailed them all together in one envelope, without any receipts or amounts. The cards were mixed together so I had to go through each one and compare the card #s to the #s in my orders. I also had to separately order envelopes. And in case anyone is wondering, the card measures 5" x 6.5", so "4x6" or A6 envelopes are too small (technically the envelope measures 4.75" x 6.5").  "5x7" or A7 envelopes should fit best.

I often use Amazon's Gift Card Balance Reload to finish the remaining balances on any prepaid debit cards I have. Those cards come from many sources these days, such as rebates, promotional offers, and sometimes even government payments. It can be difficult to use up a card's entire balance through random online/retail purchases, so I'm very thankful that Amazon allows reloading of an exact value down to the cent - no more throwing away cards that still have a small amount left. There's always something great to buy on Amazon, so these balance reloads never go to waste. Five stars.

This feature is one of my favorite things on Amazon. At first I had no clue why I would purchase a gift card on Amazon in my own account. Then I got one of those "rebate gift cards" from an item I purchased and had to figure out what to do with a Visa or MC that had only $20, $30, $40 on it. I certainly didn't want to carry it around or track how much I had spent and how much was left.

That is where the gift card balance reload feature on Amazon came to the rescue. I was able to register my rebate card with Amazon, purchase a gift card for the exact balance left on the card, wait for confirmation, and then toss the card, never to worry about it again. This is too easy!

Overall, anything that makes life just a bit simpler or more convenient is a plus. Amazon hit the nail on the head by allowing us to use gift cards, rebate cards and more to purchase Amazon "bucks" that we can use to actually get something we want. Thank you Amazon!

We all know that the best thing that you can give someone is a personalized gift to show that you care. But there are times that you also know that some individuals would appreciate an Amazon gift card, if simply because they're saving up for something very special, and a few of these allow them to reach that goal.

This [[ASIN:B00ADR3HS2 Amazon.com Gift Card - In a Gift Box]] turned out to be perfect for the individual for whom it was picked, and for last minute gifting, the free one-day shipping helps. Just be aware that there's a barcode on the back that tells the recipient "To redeem, visit amazon.com/redeemgift" so it could take away from the surprise if the person is somewhat inquisitive.

I've used these Amazon Gift Cards quite a bit over the years in a number of the variations, by email, print at home, as mail in a greeting card and even as a post to Facebook. They can vary by style and denomination, so just pick the one that's most appropriate for the recipient and go for it.

The [[ASIN:B00ADR3HS2 Amazon.com Gift Cards - In a Gift Box]] is a good one as a primary for that tough one on your gift list, or as a supplementary gift, and from personal experience, both as one who has given them and as a recipient, they are highly appreciated.

I was having trouble using a prepaid debit card for a purchase, as Amazon tried to pay the full amount of a purchase with it. The purchase amount was higher than the balance and I couldn't find a way to have Amazon use up the debit card balance first, and put the remainder on my credit card.

Solution? Buy an Amazon Gift Card for the remaining balance on the debit card. Then make your purchase, with Amazon using your gift card(s) by default, then your next payment method as is standard.

What surprised me the most was that I could buy a gift card for a nonstandard amount, which allowed me to use up the entire debit card.

I am re-reviewing this product since Amazon pulled the option (which is the same item: Fandango Gift Card) that had all the negative reviews.

Buyer beware! Fandango created a business to make it easier to purchase movie tickets online. The idea is good, but there are so many opportunities for things to go wrong and Fandango Customer Service is beyond terrible. The chances of getting burned by buying this product is very high. Since Fandango is a separate company than the theaters they deal with, you have to deal directly with Fandango if there are any issues with your tickets- the theaters cannot (and will not) help you. Fandango redeemed a $50 gift card we had and never issued tickets; when we called customer service, we were on hold for almost an hour (during which time the movie we were trying to see started). After having no luck on the phone, I filed an online complaint. It took several days for them to get back to me (in the meantime, I called again the next day and was able to get through after about a 20 minute wait). Fandango refused to issue a refund because their policy is that refunds need to be requested before the start of the show. I'd like to know how that is even possible if customer service reps don't answer the phone and don't respond to emails for days. They figure people redeeming gift certificates won't push the issue since it was a gift (not their money). There are hundreds of negative reviews about this company. Do some research before purchasing. Movie gift cards are nice, but I'd suggest going through the actual theater so that someone can assist if something goes wrong. Fandango just wants to steal your money.

Kohl's $50. Gift Card:  It was a great gift for a coworker because there are multiple Kohl's nearby. Recipient reported no problems using the card. As with any gift cards, I recommended placing a discrete identifying mark on them, so that a cashier can't (accidentally) claim that you have a $0 balance and then hand you back a different card that REALLY is empty. Keep them in sight for the entire transaction if at all possible!

Jack in the Box $25. Gift Card:  These were well received by burger-chomping friends. Recipients reported no problems using the cards. As with any gift cards, I recommended placing a discrete identifying mark on them, so that a cashier can't (accidentally) claim that you have a $0 balance and then hand you back a different card that REALLY is empty. This is especially true for drive throughs when the card you present is sometimes out of your sight.

Wendy's $25. Gift Card:  It was a great gift for coworkers because there's a Wendy's close to the office. Recipients reported no problems using the cards. As with any gift cards, I recommended placing a discrete identifying mark on them, so that a cashier can't (accidentally) claim that you have a $0 balance and then hand you back a different card that REALLY is empty. This is especially true for drive throughs when the card you present is sometimes out of your sight.

Panda Express $25. Gift Card:  I was a great gift for coworkers who like the Panda. Recipients reported no problems using the cards. As with any gift cards, I recommended placing a discrete identifying mark on them, so that a cashier can't (accidentally) claim that you have a $0 balance and then hand you back a different card that REALLY is empty. This is especially true for drive throughs when the card you present is sometimes out of your sight.

El Pollo Loco Gift Card:  The card itself was great. But twice, different drive throughs told me that the balance was $0 when I KNEW it wasn't. Both times I had them re-check. And both times the correct balance was then noted. I don't know if it was a card reader issue or something else. Regardless, I suggest marking  your card to know immediately if a different, truly $0. balance, card is handed back to you.

Amazon eGift Cards are a great and convenient gift to send to anyone, especially when we can't be with them in person for a birthday or any special occasion. It's easy to purchase, it's customizable, it is sent by email, and it arrives fast (which is great for when we only remember someone's birthday at the last minute).
Another great feature is that, since 2005, Amazon gift cards do NOT expire. All money/pre-paid gift cards (such as Visa, AE and others) always have an expiration date and, some of them, even start charging a monthly maintenance fee a few months before the expiration the date. For this reason, the first time I received a money/pre-paid gift card as a gift, I purchased an Amazon eGift Card for myself and, from then on, I always use money cards I receive to reload my Amazon eGift Card so I don't have to worry about expiration dates or about not losing the money card, and don't even need to keep track of my Amazon eGift card number because my Amazon eGift Card balance is always automatically available on my Amazon account every time I am check out a purchase and I don't need to enter any card number.

I created a gift account for myself when I returned an item. I decided that I would continue to charge my credit card directly for gifts that I bought for other people or large items that I bought for myself. However, for Kindle books and for physical books that I bought, either for myself or for gifts, it was convenient to let the gift account be charged. Sometimes, the Kindle books cost only a few dollars. Having a bunch of small dollar items on my credit card bill can be a hassle to confirm when I get my credit card bill, so I like having them be paid by the gift account. If I want to verify the history of the gift account, that information is available when I log on to Amazon. I especially like that the money will never be lessened by service charges the way that many store gift cards are reduced in value over time.

They want me on that familiar checkout page 5-10 times a day.... but seriously, why force your "gift card reload" process through the same process as making a purchase. It is NOT intuitive compared to "add funds" to a digital balance on basically any other consumer app/service (e.g., Starbucks). While my mouse was hovering over the button to complete my purchase, I had a moment of genuine pause wondering if I had been phished into clicking the wrong link and was about to order a fake product listing called "Amazon Reload"... that's how confusing this is.

To their credit, the reload process worked, and I got my $12 loaded within 5 minutes. Two stars for it technically working.

The main reason I reload my gift card is so that I can pre-order items and it works wonderfully. When you pre-order on Amazon with a credit card you are not charged until the item ships, which can lead to this back and forth waiting game that is flat out stressful. Instead, I reload my gift card with the amount total and take it out of there. This was the item is paid for and I don't have to worry about the money coming out of my bank account later, should I forget or the item ship earlier than intended. Seriously, if you pre-order anything on Amazon use the gift card feature, it will save you later.

I "reloaded" from a paypal account where I had some credit. It took a couple of tries/several attempts to figure out how to transfer the balance over (not really sure the confusion was Amazon though; it seemed like Paypal was causing the confusion). I finally got it transferred over, and everything was good. Only other issue I had was that the Amazon gift card balance was automatically "credited" or "deducted" from my next purchase. The first time I tried to purchase something, I caught this, and unchecked it, but then I forgot something so I backed out before finishing, went back and did more shopping, added something else to my cart, and then checked out. This second time checking out, I thought the gift card balance was unchecked, but I honestly didn't pay much attention since I had already gone through those steps (and all I did was add another product to my cart), so this second time I went much more quickly. Only after reviewing the order a day later (when I went to check on something), did I realize that Amazon had again automatically credited the entire gift card balance to the order. Not a huge deal, just a little annoying. Otherwise, everything went great, and I have had no other issues/complaints.

Starbucks distributed 5,000 of these cards at a cost of $450.00 each ($50 to make the card with $400 preloaded) The limited edition card sold out a week prior to release and immediately went up for sale on sites such as eBay and Amazon for $650.00 to $5000.00 each. Some were being sold minus the $400.00 pre-loaded credit, which means the money was transferred to another Starbucks card and an empty steel debit card was being sold.  Then there is always the shipping fee for something that should be shipped free of charge. I am the first to admit my Starbucks addiction and yes, I considered shelling out $450.00 for the steel embossed card, rationalizing I was really only spending 50.00 for the production cost because over the course of a year, I would spend $400.00 on their coffee anyway. But, seeing the greed of the second hand market that sprung up immediately on the card's release has disgusted me. Smooth business move on the part of Starbucks corporation limiting supply to create demand and an instant $2,000,000 profit.

We ordered several of these for gifts at Christmas.  They were delivered 'next day', without fail, even up to the day before Christmas Eve.  This one is super cute, but they have ones that are more elegant too.  The card came firmly attached so you didn't need an envelope, but it could be inserted into one if you wanted.  They look really nice just stuck in the branches of your tree until gift giving time.  For this particular one & one of another design, we were not able to have Christmas with the recipients, so I was going to need to mail them out of state.  I was hesitant about it, so I text our granddaughter-in-law, & ask if she'd like them mailed, or rather I just give her the numbers for hers & our grandsons, & see if she could add them to their Amazon account.  We decided to try texting the numbers.  I took pictures of the cards (just as they came to me), then I pulled the card, removed a strip from the back that covered the activation number, & sent them to her.  Within 5 minutes, I got a return text that they were successfully added to their account.  A little later, she excitedly text she had already made a purchase.  So the reason I shared is, I'm saving the little Santa, & the other card holder to use next year for gift card giving. 😊

Please do not purchase these gift cards on Amazon. Go locally to a retail store and purchase, so your card can be activated and you can get a receipt.

We purchase three of these as gifts. The description explained "This card is non-reloadable. No expiration of funds. No cash or ATM access. Cards are shipped ready to use." First, they do have an expiration date. Second, they do not come ready to use. THEY ARE NOT ACTIVATED.

I felt terrible. One of the people we gave one of these cards to, went to the store to use it. The store stated it had never been activated and insinuated they had stolen it. It was awful for them. Thankfully they called us right away and we were able to stop the other 2 families from trying to use theirs.

I contacted Amazon. They did do the right thing and refunded our money so we were able to replace these cards with legitimate ones. We contacted the number on the cards and confirmed that all 3 were never activated. VISA stated that the seller has to activate the cards.

Please be warned. I don't know what is going on right now with these Gift Cards and Amazon, but do not purchase.

The bad:
1. It will not arrive the next day.. tracking says to be delivered and crap but no.. It arrived the day after it was supposed to and i kept waiting for it impatiently.. so if you plan to give it as a birthday gift it might ruin your surprise..

2. The personal message that one writes during check out comes written on a small slip of paper enclosed with the package (not pasted on the box or anything).. so it gets difficult for people to guess who sent it unless they find that slip.. (this in a way is a good surprise thing too)..

All in all.. sweet and surprising gift for your dear ones..

I had a small stack of used giftcards sitting around. You know, when the balance gets to like $2.34, and you don't know what to do with them? Easy to "recharge gift balance" with all of those cards sitting around!

1. Look up the balance.
2. Click Account & Lists
3. Select Gift Cards
4. Click the yellow "Reload Your Balance" button
5. Enter Gift Card balance under the $(Other) block.
6. Click Buy Now
7. Under Payment Method, Select Change and enter gift card information.
8. Verify and click "Place Order"

You can repeat with all of your applicable gift cards. Just remember to go through and clean up your payment methods when you are done, so you don't have 13 random debit cards to choose from in the future.

Buying gifts for certain family and friends can be a pain. It seems like every time I get them a game, or something they would like they either do not like it anymore, or they already own it. I ended up deciding to get a Bahama Breeze card instead because it is something I know they would like, that they can use, and that will make them happy. I mean who does NOT love food? This gift card was perfect for that. It came quickly, and they had no problem using it. Check out with the card was simple, and they were able to get exactly what they wanted. I am happy that Amazon provides the ability to get these cards so that I know no matter what, I am able to get something for that anyone will use and enjoy. I know that some people think that a gift card is impersonal, or not something that you put a lot of thought into, however I think that if you pick something they will love and use it is great. Plus, I was able to include a little note telling them exactly why I got it for them which made it a little more personal than just handing them cash. I will be buying gift cards again through Amazon since the process was so easy and streamlined, and since I experienced no problems with my purchase, or the recipient using the card. I would suggest buying gift cards through Amazon to my friends, and my family without a hesitation.

UPDATE: 5/31/16

After all the "hulabaloo" about an empty Noodles & Co. gift card, I found out from Noodles corporate office customer setvice that you CANNOT enter all the numbers on the back of the gift card to check the balance...only the last 14 numbers from the card. As it turns out, my gift card was fully loaded and ready to use. Apparently Noodles had several complaints about the issue over the last few months because they added a special note to their gift card page..."If your gift card number has 19 digits, please only enter the last 14 digits of your card number, omitting the first 5 digits.".

**If you recently gifted a Noodles & Co. gift card, please have your giftee recheck their card balance (only enter the last 14 digits of their card number, omitting the first 5 digits) or contact Noodles directly like I did. You won't regret either transaction. It would be nice if AMAZON or the gift card seller would add this info to the product description to save time & money for everyone.

The web page is: [...]

RUNNING LOW? Check your gift card balance anytime! Enter your gift card ID number found on the back of your card in the box below and click on the "Submit" button. If it's time to fill up, you can refuel at your neighborhood Noodles & Company.

If your gift card number has 19 digits, please only enter the last 14 digits of your card number, omitting the first 5 digits.

TIP: Do join the eClub rewards. You can get free food and get coupons by email. It's worth the extra little bit of time plus you will learn about new offerings and menu changes. I especially love the BOGO coupons. I like to buy a salad and get a second one for the next day or share with a friend. Gift the card another look.

NOTE: I was not compensated for my review.

First off I don't remember the last time I wrot a review since I only belive about 10% of what I read but I got so sick I felt I needed to do something so here you go. Stayed next door at a hotel so it was convenient to walk next door for four of us to eat dinner. The hotel and the restaurant were very new so it looked like a good fit. The restaurant was not very busy at all which made me wonder why. Maybe it was a little out of the way or too new for locals to find.I ordered a steak and I believe my steak was contaminated by someone or it was just to old to eat. Anyway this is what I sent back to the restaurant chain hoping they will react to what I experienced that night after eating my dinner in Landcaster, Pa.
To the restaurant chain:
I Ordered a sirloin steak medium rare and believe I received a filet cooked rare. Ate it anyway after the server assured me it was a sirloin even though it was about 3" in diameter and 2" thick, rare meat is usually ok with me rather than medium. A few hours later I got the chills and shakes that I could not fight off and went to bed about 8:00PM, at 4:30 AM thi next morning I woke up violently ill with nausea, diahria, body aches and a fever that I believe was attributed to the piece of meat I ate at your restaurant. This was a new restaurant that was sparsely occupied so I believe I was served a piece of meat that was old and contaminated. I was sick for 60 hours as a result of eating that steak dinner.

After redesigning a workflow process, I think everyone will agree that it should be easier and less confusing. Amazon has managed to do the opposite!

- It now takes 5 clicks to add money to an existing gift card! Why?
- It's now more laborious to do multiple reloads in a row. The old process had a link at the end of the process to "reload again". The new process does not have such a link.
- It now appears like you are buying something from another company, which is super confusing and made me wonder if I was even doing the right thing.
- It now asks me to confirm whether I really want to use the debit card that I always use to purchase my gift card reloads. Yes already! Another un-necessary click to confirm every single time.

Usually Amazon has good user experience and workflow. Major fail here, Amazon.

Here are a couple benefits to buying an Amazon gift card I don't think everybody thinks of. I read it before on some article about uses for Amazon gift cards and I think they are brilliant ideas.

Before I share my first one, let me start by saying that I don't remember how I lived to before Amazon and I don't want to try to remember it. It's kind of a similar feeling to when I think about how people must've lived before the Internet and how hard it must have been to have to go look things up at the library instead of googling them. So my first use for an Amazon gift card is actually just to build up a gift card amount to use to purchase my many many products that I am constantly making orders for on Amazon instead of using a credit card or debit card for each of those orders that early. The reason for that being that I ended up with line after line and page after page of Amazon orders on my credit card which made it really difficult to find any other purchases on there, most importantly possibly purchases that I did not make. My credit card has been stolen several times and the only way I found out was because I happened to see a purchase on The statement that was not familiar. Virtually impossible to do that now one almost everything says Amazon on my credit card bill. So instead I use an Amazon gift card to buy everything on Amazon and that leaves my credit card clear of Amazon plastered all over the statement.

The second thing that I use it for is actually just to get rid of other odds and ends of gift cards such as other visa gift cards and things like that that you get as a gift. I do that because usually End up with two dollars and change or so on on each of my leftover Visa gift cards and I have nothing to do with it because you can't spend it without knowing the exact balance and you have to remember that Number as you go to buy something andmake sure you're not applying to something that's more than what you have left on your card and it just turns into a huge pain. So instead of trying to use it from the visa gift card itself, I just buy an Amazon gift card with that which you can buy in any dollar amount and you can use a Visa gift card to purchase it, therefore getting rid of your visa gift card while using every single penny on it.

Those are my two biggest uses for Amazon gift cards and this hundred dollar gift card I got because Amazon was offering five dollars to you if you purchased $100 or more and Amazon gift cards, so why not? I know I probably spend that with in a month, if not a week :)

I bought this for our family's Christmas white elephant gift exchange this year. I put this with a big tub of Lysol wipes in a gift bag. It seemed fitting for 2020. I wanted to gift something that anyone could use, and an Amazon giftcard seemed like the best way to do that. My husband gifted the new Amazon Echo dot, and honestly, our gifts were the only one's that were traded! The gift card was traded the most at 4 times, so I felt good knowing that the giftcard was a good way to go this year. I think we all can use one right now.

This giftcard came in a sealed plastic wrapper, so you know that it's not been tampered with. There's also a receipt that shows the giftcard amount and I recommend that this should be put with the giftcard when gifting, just in case any issues arise when they're redeeming. My dad actually ended up with the giftcard, so I will update if he has any issues. Our giftcard box came in good shape. It moved up & down smoothly and didn't have any noticeable creases anywhere in person. The one thing I wish it had was the giftcard amount listed on the giftcard itself. Aside from the receipt, there's no amount listed anywhere, so we had to write $20 on the back with a marker. Not the most presentable way, but we needed something that could be seen easily so the white elephant game could continue with ease. I seen that other reviewer's giftcards had the total on the upper corner of the giftcard itself, so that's what I was expecting. Amazon giftcards ordered online rarely have that, so I wasn't completely expecting it, but hoping this one would be different. This is one thing that would be a great addition to all Amazon giftcards. I've ordered one from almost every listing on here, and that's the one thing that's been a bit aggravating each time. I folded the receipt and stuffed it behind the black giftcard. These are not the shiny plastic giftcards. These, like almost all of the giftcards bought on here, are a thick card stock material. Most people probably don't care if they're just going to load &dispose, but I prefer the plastic ones personally, because I like to save mine up. There's really not much else to say about a giftcard. I hope my experience and pictures help give you an idea of what to expect in person. Please let me know if this helps. It gives me that little encouragement to continue to take the time to share my experiences.

I got this $10 giftcard as part of a bundle promotion during Cyber Monday. It came sealed in a plastic bag so you know it hasn't been tampered with. The black envelope comes glued closed, as well, which makes it great for gifting. This does have $10 on it, and was a nice incentive. If you're purchasing this outright to gift, I hope these pictures also help give you an idea of what to expect in person. I know there are many amazon gift card options to choose from, and I've bought many, so I just wanted to show what this particular gift card looks like in person. FYI...This is the hard cardboard style giftcard, which I've noticed is what seems to come in most options. Even the one's that come in the fancier box options on here. This is shinier. Some are matte. Most people probably don't care, but just thought I'd mention it.

*Tips if looking because of a bundle promo: Don't forget to add this in your cart along with the accompanying item if this is offered as a promotion. But...You must get to this page by clicking on the promotional add or it will not count. I originally tried just searching for a $10 gift card and adding it, but it would not deduct the discount when doing it that way. The link should be on the product page. Just click the words giftcard, and it should bring you here. I personally got mine as part of the Ring doorbell pro &Chime pro bundle on Cyber Monday. I actually thought it was going to be emailed for some reason, but it arrived in the box with the Chime Pro. I hope this review helps someone in some way.

I bought two of these from my daughter's volleyball team to their coaches for our volleyball banquet. In past years they've always received some type of volleyball item, and me and the other mom's thought we'd get them $50 gift cards instead so they can buy themselves whatever they want. I chose the gift box option, because it looks very classy and comes ready to gift if you want. I personally chose to put these in a little gift bag for a little mystery, and the fact they came with boxes really made this idea work.

They quality of the packaging was beautiful. The box has a beveled Amazon printed allover the entire top lid, including the sides. I do wish that the actual gift cards themselves were plastic like a credit card or how most other gift cards usually are. These are a thick stock paper/cardboard material. It still works, and for our purpose, I was happy. I would suggest getting a plastic option that doesn't come with a box if you're going to just unbox it and put this in an actual card. I almost bought these to do that, and figured at least I'd have a little gift box if needed. I now would recommend not doing that unless you are gifting in the box. I mean, you still could. It's just a gift card after all. The one meant for placing in a card will look higher quality for that purpose. But for handing someone a card with this gift box accompanying it would be a great idea, too.

This came in a sealed plastic bag to protect it, and so you can tell it hasn't been tampered with. It has an instruction card, which I placed in the lid, as well as a receipt incase there's any issues, which I folded and put under the gift cards. This is my experience, and I hope this helps with your gifting ideas and needs. I hope my pictures help give you an idea of to expect in person.

The $50 gift card was part of a deal that if you bought Office 365 for $100 you got a $50 amazon gift card, pretty good deal so I bought it. I received the office 365 code soon after order and then a few days later I received the $50 amazon gift card in the mail. I didn't redeem it right away as I forgot about it. Eventually, it resurfaced a few weeks later and when I tried to redeem it I kept getting errors entering the claim code. Problem was the card that was shipped to me was not the one that amazon system's show as the one that was activated and shipped to my address. In you order history it shows the gift card Serial number, the one on my order history ended with S/N 79, the one I received had all the same digits except the last two, which were 81. So the person who put my envelope together must've grabbed the wrong card when shipping these out. Took three separate chat attempts and photos of the card I received to get Amazon to believe me since someone else received the $50 gift card and used the credit.

I use these gift cards to consolidate the remaining balance on pre-paid credit cards into a single account balance because Amazon won't let you use multiple cards for a single purchase. Just use your old PP credit card to purchase a gift card for yourself for the CC balance and redeem it into your Amazon account. Then Amazon will apply your account balance to any new purchase and you can use your new PP credit card to pay the remaining amount. It's the best method I've found to get rid of old PP credit card balances.

This will likely be my last review  ever- plan to really go out of my way to avoid buying anything from Amazon in the future. I Ordered this product on the basis of a "guaranteed next day delivery" for a friend's birthday.  I took a screenshot of of webpage showing the guarantee screen. The next day, checking status, Amazon's website tracking indicates a delay of 2 extra days - 3 day delivery instead of next day. I called up Amazon expecting some kind of help on expediting the shipment, or at least giving me the option to switch it over to an electronic gift certificate.  Instead, I spent an hour with different call center folks in India trying to smooth talk me into accepting that there is no option but to wait for deliver, that they could not cancel and re-issue the order. Basically they provides a series of hollow apologies but didn't lift a finger. What a load of BS... I told them that if I cancelled the order and placed a new one , my gift would get there faster than the "best that amazon support could do for me". At this point, they said they coudn't cancel the order either. I asked to speak to a supervisor and here's where I was extremely disappointed to hear exactly the same thing. When I asked them what the definition of the guarantee was, I got absolute silence... Not only that, but the supervisor seemed to be annoyed at the question itself. They would not lift a finger to help me and didn't offer anything. After pressing the supervisor on the fact that I placed my order with Amazon on the basis of  guarantee and an expectation that a birthday present would arrive on birthday, she finally said the best she could do was give me one month free on my prime subscription. I realized these people were only going to delay me further.  I declined and informed the supervisor that I wanted to cancel the order immediately. She at least helped me with this cancellation....  Immediately afterwards, I  ordered an apple store e-gift certificate in  a few seconds, problem solved. Friend happy. True, it was not in the pretty package I had planned for... but at least it made it on time. From my perspective, Amazon had previously been a great vendor, the A to Z guarantee was honored by them in several occasions, great support. But obviously things there are going downhill. So long Amazon.

I originally bought these thinking it was 1 gift card with $5 balance, since it costs $5. I since know better and ended up keeping them. I activated one as a quick gift to a friend, it was very easy to activate and the little box it comes in makes it a little special and festive. All that aside, I REALLY STRONGLY believe these should be FREE.
1. Many Visa gift cards(which you can spend anywhere!) are free to buy at the store
2. When someone gifts an AMAZON gift card, amazon will benefit, they will get the money, and probably more business.
3. The box is pretty, but cardboard, it's not the nothing you would pay $1.66 at the store.
So with all those reasons in consideration, it give it 3 stars.

In conclusion, from your faithful Amazon Prime members, please consider giving these for FREE, it's really in your favor.

We contacted Amazon immediately and they assured me there would not be a problem. Still they gave me a one time only refund and told me to go ahead and try using them as the Amazon rep was certain the cards were still valid. The gift was for a relative who lives 200 miles away so we told them the situation and asked them to try the cards at a local LaRosa's. Sure enough, the cards were NOT redeemable because they lacked the authentication code. Buyer Beware when purchasing gift cards from Amazon for other businesses.

They warn that this gift card only works for Premium subscribers, and that it doesn't work for Student or Family plans. What they DON'T warn you is that if you have Premium with free Hulu (from an automatic promotion some time back), the gift card also doesn't work. I had to cancel my subscription and go to the Free plan, wait for the current month of subscription to expire, then go in and redeem the gift card and sign up for a year of Premium. Nothing on their website or in their help pages explained this to me. I had to find and talk to customer support to find this out.

Speaking of finding and talking to customer support... I couldn't find them anywhere through the Spotify app or through their website. If you go to their support site and then click on Chat it brings you to a community forum, totally misleading and disappointing. I had to go out to Google to the actual chat support site. Even then, I had to click through a useless help article until I could find a button to open a chat window with support. They've made sure that if you need help, you have to go on a hunt to find them, even if you're a paying customer.

So I waited for my subscription to expire and revert to the Free plan, and then tried to redeem the gift card, and I STILL got a very useless error message "something went wrong, contact support". So I had to go through the above process again to get someone to help.

The amount of trial and error, searching, talking to support, waiting, and account adjustments I've had to do, just to apply this gift card, has convinced me never to ask for this gift card again. It's just not worth the effort.

Living in Georgia, we rarely get snow, especially snow that sticks. When I saw I could order a snowflake from Amazon, I was ecstatic! I placed the order immediately, and received it the next day with free overnight shipping. That's where my excitement ended. I ran to the door to greet the UPS driver, and rushed to open the box. To my dismay, the 70° weather that day was my biggest foe. Upon opening the box, I was greeted with a puddle of water. I had planned on ordering 1,000,000 more of these immediately before Christmas so we could finally have a white Christmas here, but Mother Nature isn't having any of it. Perhaps this would be better suited for the northernmost part of the United States, but alas, down here in the hot/humid south, it's just not meant to be...

On the bright side though, I was able to redeem the code in the puddle for $50 worth of Amazon goods, so it wasn't all bad!

For security reasons and for my normal volume of orders, I would only load a balance if I knew I would spend it all immediately afterwards.

Even though this gift-card-in-a-tin is designed to be a generic holiday gift, I purchased it as a birthday gift for a friend with a winter birthday because none of the Amazon birthday designs I saw appealed to me and because I liked the idea of a reusable gift card container. (I may be wrong, but I don't believe any of Amazon's birthday gift cards are packaged in tins.) The design of this one is obviously intended to be suitable for any winter holiday -- Christmas, New Year, Hanukkah (note that this snowflake has six points, like the Star of David, and is blue, the official Hanukkah color), Kwanzaa, Festivus, etc. This generic design worked in my favor because I intended to use it for a birthday gift, but if I had been shopping for a holiday gift, I would have preferred a design specific to that particular holiday.

I generally avoid giving people gift cards because I find them impersonal (as well as for other practical reasons that I won't go into here), but when I do, I prefer to present them in gift containers instead of just slipping them into cards for a couple of reasons: a package make the gift card a bit less guessable and reduces the chance that the gift card will be overlooked or discarded by mistake, as can happen when it's enclosed in a card. However, I also try to be environmentally responsible and choose packaging, like this tin, that can be re-used over and over, in place of wrapping paper that is often thrown out after a single use.

One selling feature for this Amazon card was that at the time I bought it, it came with free one-day shipping. I'm a Prime member, so I would have gotten "free" 2-day shipping anyway, but as a chronic procrastinator, every little bit helps.

Overall, I was pleased with this gift card/gift tin and thought it looked rather classy, as far as gift card boxes go, and it's likely that I'll purchase another (or similarly packaged gift card) next year.

This is not good customer service. Please notify us if there has been a policy change or if there is a "glitch" that needs correction. I am generally a very happy and loyal customer at Amazon. So I got 2 of these on a lightening deal. Sometimes (especially on lightening deal) I buy quickly and then go back and read reviews later. After I read the reviews I was terrified that I would get one that looked used (I was purchasing as a gift,) there would be nothing/only a portion on it, or the place that the person I gifted it to wanted to use it wouldn’t take it. This is all I read when first reading reviews. However, I had already purchased so there was nothing I could do at that point. Since I read such horrible reviews, I decided to keep it for myself. I didn’t want there to be a problem with the card after I gave it as a gift. I’m pleased to say that both cards looked crisp and new and I had no problem at all in using either one. I actually used one of them at multiple stores and stores that other reviewers had said the store wouldn’t take. The main thing to note is you have to use these as credit. Everywhere I used these the cashier knew just what they were and how to use them. At the end, if you have a balance left you can split the transaction so that you can use the full amount. You’ll need to know the balance but checking the amount is extremely easy. Overall, these are great to gifts because the person you are giving it to has the option to use at multiple places. Not to mention if you keep an eye out Amazon is often offering these on sale so they can be a great deal on places that you usually can’t get a deal at. I highly recommend. They really are very simple to use and are the perfect gift idea.

I'm a Mac person but Microsoft really got me with Office 365. At first I was annoyed to have a subscription service but since I'm part of a family of 5, it worked out that we got our money's worth. Office isn't a perfect application--I LOVE Excel more than Numbers and Word is more complete than Pages but can t clunkier, not organized as well, and not as good for Desktop publishing and images. But Office at this price is excellent.
The next thing I hated was that 365 switched from the number of devices to the number of users. I could STAND that my home computer that my 3 kids used had to have separate logins for applications on the same computer. BUT, when Dropbox continually refused to offer smaller monthly packages and limited the number of devices on their free version, I started to look elsewhere. Lo and behold Microsoft OneDrive was my answer. And it was ALREADY INCLUDED with my Office 365. Now everyone in my family has 1 TB of OneDrive storage and they can access it on their iphones, laptops or wherever they can get an internet connection. I can share REALLY LARGE VIDEO FILES with as many parents from the school that I want. I can share lots of school videos for the parents to download AND I don't have to store them on my computer. In other words I can have them on the cloud (OneDrive) and they don't have to sync to my computer. This frees up a lot of space on my computer. (I save copies on external drives of course.) Good-bye Dropbox!
So this has become an essential part of my life and one of the subscription services I consider a must-have along with Amazon Prime.

So while it sounds terrible to complain about a gift... I always hated it when people would give me those Visa or Discover gift cards. They are such a pain to manage and I've always had nothing but issues trying to use them. If you don' t have the full amount available on the card, some merchants won't let you use them. I would also be very careful about only using them for things that I knew I would never need to return... because what a nightmare that would be if you no longer had the card. Bottom line... I hate those things. But then a few months back, I had a revelation to simply use that Visa gift card to buy myself an Amazon Gift card (reload) and have it placed directly into my account. Regardless of the amount... whether it is some odd leftover like $23.78 or an even $100, you can load it to your Amazon gift card balance and dump those stupid Visa Gift cards. I know I'll definitely use the Amazon funds, and Amazons gift card balance management makes it incredibly convenient. Plus... some of those Visa and Discover gift cards lose value over time due to "maintenance fees." So if you're like me and hate dealing with those things, or throw them in a drawer and forget about them... do yourself a favor and convert them over to Amazon bucks. Life becomes so much easier!

I will ALWAYS have a pack of these on hand! It is completely worth the $2.99 for a 10 pack! That is $0.30 per card to have it readily available in a nice gifting sleeve and the ability to load with the amount you choose, whenever it's needed.
That being said, I understand why some have complained about the activation process with the Amazon App. To make it work, I had to download a QR scanner separate from the Amazon app. Then when I scanned the QR code on the back of the card, it opens a link in a web browser page, and I can complete the gift card loading process that way. It's easy-peasy to load and only takes a couple of minutes, but it may require a separate app.

I agree that Amazon really should get the QR scanner working for these gift cards within their app. I'm giving them 4 stars just due to the inconvenience of the Amazon App not working to activate them, and if they correct that problem I will change my review to 5 stars.

These are perfect when you realize you want to give a gift card but don't have time to go to the store. Every year I give gift cards to my office staff as presents. I love being able to activate them in the convenience of my own home and not worry about a cashier rushing and making a mistake. Also it is sometimes nerve-wracking walking back to the car with hundreds of dollars worth of gift cards. So, I have been adding a couple packs of these to all of my recent orders, just to have on hand. They are slightly slimmer than the ones you purchase in a store and thus easier to mail, as another customer mentioned.

The only downside is that the graphic design is very basic, particularly relative to this year's Target gift cards that have LED lights flashing inside of them. Hopefully Amazon will continue to offer these in the future with holiday theming!

I have found an Amazon gift card is the perfect way to remember my loved ones at special times.  Because my children are grown and live away from me as do my sisters, and many of my friends, I don't always know what they like and do not have.  But, I have conquered that by buying them a gift card.  They are always pleased with receiving it and eager to tell me how they used it which makes me so happy.  Try it, you won't be disappointed nor will those who receive it.

With children, adolescents, teens, and adults for whom I must shop for birthdays, anniversaries, and holidays, my poor brain gets overloaded with ideas and suggestions. Then, because we do not all live near one another or get to see on another regularly, I do not always have an idea of what they prefer. BUT. . .I found a sure cure way to please them all and get marvelous thank you notes!

I am willing to share with you. I send Amazon gift cards!!!!!

For those I like a lot, I send a more expensive card, for those I am just remembering, I send a less expensive one. No matter, I know that my family and friends can go to Amazon.com and find something that they will really want and with which they will be pleased.

I have had thank you notes for such varied things as towels, sheet music, transformers (that is a toy, I learned), and books. Not for a moment would I have thought of these gifts and yet they were gratefully received.

AND, your ordering them is as easy as clicking your cursor on one of the links below, entering the information such as to whom it is to go, for how much, and your credit card information. BUT. . .the GREATEST thing is, if you forget and you are driving to work and remember your mother's birthday is TODAY (YIKES!), you can go to the link and have the card delivered to her computer THAT DAY!!!!!! She can shop for her gift before you get home from work! Now, you cannot beat that.

So, I highly recommend an Amazon.com gift card to solve your shopping woes.

This seems like an odd product to review. If you are buying this, you are probably already a fan of Amazon. That said, a few thoughts.

The gift card can be had in almost any dollar amount you want, there are a variety of actual cards to choose from, and you get the usual Amazon fast-shipping. A gift card isn't the most personal of gifts, but some people really just want one, and they are a great go to when you need to give a gift to someone you don't know very well or are obligated to give something to (such as at work where a colleague is getting married, having a kid, etc).

If you ever have an issue with the gift card, such as some of the numbers being smudged, I have never had less than stellar customer service from Amazon, so getting a replacement should be a snap. All in all this is a simple, good product.

I don't even remember the machinations that I had to do to finally fix things, but it was far more of a dance than it should have been. I understand (now) that this is a common practice, and not really Amazon's fault, entirely, but since I DO have a credit card on file with them, and I HAVE had an account with them for quite a number of years, you'd think they would have a mechanism to avoid a customer annoyance such as this. For my grandchildren, these Palmer Gold Chocolate Coins with gift card or money holder packaging will be "almost" as exciting as finding the Golden Ticket on Charlie and the Chocolate Factory! Of course, I had to open a package of the chocolate coins myself, which tastes like, well, chocolate. They remind me of the chocolate coins I used to get as a kid.
Though bags of chocolate coins are readily available, these are the first chocolate coins I've ever seen that open up to, hopefully, reveal some real spendable cash. In my grandkids case, they will be holding real money, gift cards, and probably some Monopoly money, just to make them a little more fun.
These chocolate coin money holders are very well-made, the chocolate tastes pretty good, and they are so much fun! These will make great stocking stuffers, party favors, and good behavior or chore chart rewards, etc. I will be making these up as the Treasures for a Christmas scavenger hunt.
I think these are yummy, great fun, and can be used in many creative ways. I would absolutely recommend them!

Gift cards are simply the best option for allowing the recipient to buy what they need, taking guesswork out of planning and gifting people for occasions. Unless they have a public wishlist on Amazon or you are making a custom DIY gift for the recipient, you might very well buy a gift that isn't a useful to them, specially if you don't know their likes and dislikes.

Therefore in the time when buying random physical gifts are obsolete, fancy gift cards need to be given a special place in terms of customizable presentation, designs, colors and styles - specially on a platform like Amazon. You would think that you will be spoiled with various beautiful and upend choices of gift cards packaged in great variety of styles and designs. But to my astonishments, the range of choices are very limited, often to very cheap and dollar store type thrown together packagings. Neither are environmentally friendly, using chemical heavy processes to produce gloss and sparkles.

Another option is to go with a plain card design and then get a separate gift wrapping and boxing solutions. A search for that yielded few and poor selection of results.

This seemed somewhat better than others but apart from the dreadful faults shown in other reviews such as the card arriving bent and with faults, no thought has been given to the process;

(1) The box arrives inside an ugly, cheap nylon packaging with a sticker label that reveals ALL the serial numbers and information necessary to use the gift card without even opening it. How dumb is that. This can open the gift card to theft as you don't even have to open the box to use the code to claim the value.

I wanted to open the box and inspect it to be sure all is as should be and adding a few more customized items to it. You couldn't do that because of that stupid and dumb nylon packaging. Why not open the nylon packaging then, you might ask. Since I didn't have a prior experience with this design, I didn't know if I could open the nylon package since the critical information regarding the code were on the seal line that closed the box. I imagined and hoped the information is also printed on the card but wasn't sure since there were references in reviews and other places that nothing is printed on the cards. They are just cosmetic and generic. I know this might sound a no brainer, but given how often these things are overlooked, I wanted to be sure. I opened the box and the information was on the card's back but then I still don't understand why you would print it on a nylon packaging that the unit came in; something that a user should throw away. A blank and clear nylon packaging was sufficient and would avoid creating confusion.

Also rather than a nylon, I preferred an eco friendly packing option such as a cardboard paper of some sort.

(2) Dumber still, the value on the card is not printed. This was a customized amount not listed among options. This is for making it easier for the supplier not having to bother with an additional step of printing something. Nevertheless, that sometimes matter and important to the giver and receiver. Most cards you buy from the shelves also do not have a value printed unless you buy the pre-loaded ones at set values. The point of getting a customized card is to have it customized.

(3) The last but not least is the no return, no refund policy. If a card's style or presentation doesn't meet expectations, they should make it easier to cancel and transfer the funds to another style or replacement.

So to protect my money but still contribute to the economy, I use credit cards for all of my buying needs, but that doesn't help me get the higher interest rate on my account. So I buy 10 of these cards at $1 each every month. The money goes into my amazon account which we all know that I will spend it sooner or later AND THEN I get my higher interest yields for doing so. So yea, I get paid to buy these every month in the form of higher interest rates from my bank.

I'm trying to help my 72 year old mom save some money since she's on a fixed income but still thinks money grows on trees. She is not tech savvy and hates change, especially when it comes to anything that involves a computer, TV, cell phone, etc. (she still owns and uses a VHS player). I purchased her a Roku and decided to get her this Netflix gift card so she could see if this was something she could possibly use to cut down her cable costs without making any long-term commitment. I helped her set everything up (okay, I set everything up), showed her how simple it was to use, the HUGE variety of shows Netflix offers AND no commercials. Well, this might be the best purchase I've ever made for her. She loves it and is obsessed with Netflix! It frees up some additional time for me because she's 'too busy watching her shows' to need me to come over and help with her 87 projects. Win win! Using the card was easy - no problems and new subscribers still get their first month free! She's already had me add her debit card so when the gift card runs out, she'll still have all of 'her shows'.

Received my card in two days, it was even delivered by my mailman on a Sunday. My mom loves gardening (she spends at least six hours a day in her yard since retiring), so this will be a great gift for Mother's Day. I just wish these cards were offered in smaller denominations ($50.00 is the least expensive card available unless you want to do an email gift certificate) and I've already purchased other gifts for her - was hoping to add a $25.00 card and an email gift certificate would not be ideal since she RARELY checks her email. I wanted an actual gift card to give her with her other gifts.
